english: Cs.; rnág-par (rnag par) rnág-pa (rnag pa) to form pus, to ulcerate Cs. - skráṅs-pa (skrangs pa) rnág-tu (rnag tu) °kug ('kug) v. °gug-pa ('gug pa). - rnag-khrág (rnag khrag) matter and blood. - rnág-ċan (rnag can) containing pus, purulent. - rnag-brúm (rnag brum) abscess Sch. - rnag-śubs (rnag shubs) prob. the core of an ulcer.
W., C., ready money, cash, *nag kyaṅ* (nag kyang) id.; *nag-zog* (nag zog) money and goods; *gir-mo gyad nag* (gir mo brgyad nag) Ld. eight rupees in cash.

rnán-ba (rnan ba) pf. brnaṅs (brnangs) to be checked, stopped, shut off; with or without gré-bar (gre bar), to stick fast in one's throat; to be choked (complete form brnaṅs-te (brnangs te) °či-ba ('chi ba)); dbúgs-kyis (dbugs kyis) rnán-śiṅ (rnan shing) (his) breath stopping short (from fright) Pth.; skád-kyis (skad kyis) rnán-te (rnan te) not being able to utter a word Dzl. 22, 1; zás-kyis (zas kyis) rnán-te (rnan te) the food sticking fast in his throat, mya-ṅán-gyis (mya ngan gyis) from sorrow Dzl.
rnam (rnam), in compounds for rnám-par (rnam par), v. rnám-par (rnam par) extr.

rnam-pa (rnam pa) 1. piece, part, e.g. the parts of a panel of a door, *riṅ-gi nám-pa* (ring gi nam pa) a longitudinal piece, *żéṅ-gi nám-pa* (zheng gi nam pa) a cross piece W.; rnám-pa (rnam pa) gnyís-su (gnyis su) gyes (gyes) (a ray of light) is divided into two parts or rays; section, distinct part of a treatise; part, ingredient, lús-kyi (lus kyi) rnám-pa (rnam pa) ṗrá-rags-rnams (phra rags rnams) the subtile and the coarse ingredients of the body Wdn.; rnám-pa (rnam pa) kún-tu (kun tu), tams-čád-du (thams cad du) in every respect, to all intents and purposes, through and through, entirely, perfectly; this phrase is used, whenever people of rank are addressed: rnam-kún (rnam kun) ṫúgs-rje (thugs rje) mgo-°drén (mgo 'dren) bka-drin (bka' drin) mṫsuṅs-brál (mtshungs bral) most honoured patron, altogether incomparable as to grace and goodness! or, rnam-kún (rnam kun) ṫúgs-rje (thugs rje) daṅ (dang) bka-drin (bka' drin) mṫsuṅs-brál (mtshungs bral); European gentlemen are thus addressed in letters: rnam-kún (rnam kun) ṫúgs-rje (thugs rje) °gyur-méd ('gyur med) sá-heb (sa heb) most honoured Sahib, invariably kind in every respect! - 2. things or persons taken individually, often pleon., °od-zér ('od zer) rnám-pa (rnam pa) bżi (bzhi) four (separate) rays of light; jó-bo (jo bo) rnam(-pa) (rnam pa) gnyís (gnyis) the two lords (sc. gods) Glr.; bdag (bdag) °dir ('dir) tsogs (tshogs) bú-mo (bu mo) rnám-pa (rnam pa) lṅa (lnga) we five girls here assembled Mil.; *sá-heb nám-pa nyi* (sa heb nam pa nyi) W. the two European gentlemen; čo-°ṗrúl (cho 'phrul) rnám-pa (rnam pa) bčo-brgyád (bco brgyad) the eighteen wonderful feats; °byúṅ-ba ('byung ba) rnám-pa (rnam pa) lṅa (lnga) Wdn. the five elements; żal-zás (zhal zas) rnám-pa (rnam pa) Dzl. v. S, 17 the separate dishes of a meal (another reading: żal-zás-rnams (zhal zas rnams)); when used in quite a general sense, the exact meaning is to be understood only by the context: lhá-sa (lha sa) rnam-pa (rnam pa) gnyís (gnyis) tsár-nas (tshar nas) after finishing the two Lhasa affairs, viz. the erecting of two buildings previously mentioned; rnám-pa (rnam pa) ṫams-čád (thams cad) mkyén-pai (mkhyen pa'i) ye-śés (ye shes) S.O., or spyan (spyan) Dzl., as much as omniscience; gzugs (gzugs) ni (ni) ḱa-dóg (kha dog) daṅ (dang) dbyibs-kyi (dbyibs kyi) rnám-pao (rnam pa'o) 'gzugs' (gzugs) is that in which both colour and form are included Wdn. - 3. division, class, species, dpuṅ (dpung) rnam (rnam) bżi (bzhi) the four species of troops (cavalry, elephants, chariots, infantry); rnám-pa (rnam pa) bżi (bzhi) of four different kinds. - 4. manner, way, rnám-pa (rnam pa) sna-ṫsógs-kyis (sna tshogs kyis), rnám-pa (rnam pa) sna-ṫsógs-kyi (sna tshogs kyi) sgó-nas (sgo nas) in manifold manner, variously, frq.; rnám-pa (rnam pa) drúg-tu (drug tu) (the earth shakes) in six ways, i.e. directions (whenever extraordinary works of charity are performed by holy men) v. Burn. I., 262 (not 'six times' Sch.); rnám-pas (rnam pas) = sgó-nas (sgo nas), or ṗyir (phyir), bslú-bai (bslu ba'i) rnám-pas (rnam pas) by arts of seduction Dzl.; dé-la (de la) mi (mi) dgá-bai (dga' ba'i) rnám-pas (rnam pas) from vexation at it Mil.; bsér-mai (bser ma'i) rnám-pas (rnam pas) in consequence of the cold wind Mil. - 5. outward appearance, exterior, as to form, figure, shape: lčágs-kyui (lcags kyu'i) rnám-pa (rnam pa) in the shape of a hook, hooked Wdn.; stón-pai (ston pa'i) rnám-par (rnam par) sprul (sprul) he assumed the appearance of the Teacher Tar.; čós-skui (chos sku'i) rnám-par (rnam par) °gyúr-ba ('gyur ba) to appear in a misty form Glr.; lus (lus) °di ('di) ni (ni) roi (ro'i) rnám-par (rnam par) °gyur ('gyur) this body turns into a corpse Thgy., and so in most cases with regard to the whole appearance; of colour alone it is used only, when dbyibs (dbyibs) (the shape) has already been stated, as in a passage from Pth.: as to its rnám-pa (rnam pa) (colour), it is spotted like a leopard; deportment, demeanour, gesture, yíd-du (yid du) °óṅ-bai ('ong ba'i) rnám-pas (rnam pas) of graceful manners Mil.; further: state, manner of existence, of certain inhabitants of hell Thgy.; in philosophical writings: 'Form der Erkenntniss' Was. (274); men-
